mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced household consumption, which compresses corporate earnings and drives negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in greater than 55 percent of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, leading to negative forward equity returns as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts yield positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the index of industrial production above 0.5 percent signals robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization that supports corporate profitability, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth exceeding 0.5 percent correlates with negative 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of observations during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 260.0, elevated inflation expectations force market participants to price in higher future discount rates, resulting in negative forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 260.0 are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and higher aggregate demand, which translates into stronger corporate revenues and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth correlates with negative 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of rolling 2-year windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: During recessionary troughs, a positive quarter-over-quarter change in real GDP combined with stabilizing retail sales signals an economic rebound, producing positive forward equity returns as risk premiums compress.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth during recessions yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production below negative 0.3 percent signals supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative short-term equity returns as earnings forecasts are cut.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines below negative 0.3 percent yield positive 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases during late-cycle phases.
